verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein that resides predominantly in the ER membrane, where its luminal EF-hand senses ER Ca2+ depletion and triggers oligomerization at ER-PM junctions to gate Orai1. However, a documented STIM1 pool reaches the plasma membrane itself, with the N-terminal EF-SAM domain exposed extracellularly. This surface STIM1 pool has been detected by surface biotinylation and antibody labeling on intact cells in multiple cell types, including platelets and some tumor lines, and antibodies against the EF-SAM region can engage it from outside. This is a minority pool alongside the dominant ER-membrane localization — classic dual_localization with a real, accessible outer-leaflet fraction by its own TM anchor.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface STIM1 pool is small and cell-type variable; whether it is sufficient for therapeutic engagement is unclear, but documented PM fraction supports contextual.
